title: PAS 6 Filing Online Service in India – Fees, Documents & Help
metaDescription: What is PAS 6 form? Get PAS 6 filing online with expert consultant support. Check documents required for PAS 6 filing, filing fees & quick service.

hero.headline: PAS 6 Filing Online in India – Consultant Support, Fees & Documents
hero.subtext: Stay compliant with Professionals Law – trusted partner for PAS-6 data, certification, and MCA filing.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Form PAS-6 is a half-yearly compliance report required to be filed by all unlisted public companies in India as per Rule 9A(8) of the Companies (Prospectus and Allotment of Securities) Rules, 2014. This report ensures that the share capital held in physical form is reconciled with the depository records, as mandated by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). The form has to be certified by a Practicing Chartered Accountant or Company Secretary and need to be filed on half yearly basis for the half-year ending on the 31st day of March and the 30th day of September. Professionals Law supports in conducting the entire process of shareholder data collection, reconciliation with NSDL/CDSL data, preparation of form PAS-6, and its filing on MCA portal to enable accurate compliance with timeline for your unlisted public company.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: The MCA has made PAS-6 filing mandatory to promote transparency and dematerialisation among unlisted public companies. This helps prevent discrepancies in shareholding records and ensures that companies are progressively converting physical shareholding into electronic form. Filing PAS-6 also ensures compliance with the Companies (Prospectus and Allotment of Securities) Rules, thereby avoiding penalties and legal complications. Professionals Law guarantees your reconciliation will be done with precision and in accordance with depository protocol to keep your company in compliance and audit ready at all times.

body.title: Enhanced Transparency
body.description: Filing PAS-6 ensures proper reconciliation between physical and electronic shareholding, enhancing the accuracy of share capital records.

body.title: Regulatory Compliance
body.description: Helps companies fulfill MCA’s dematerialisation and filing mandates, avoiding legal non-compliance issues.

body.title: Improved Shareholder Trust
body.description: Builds confidence among shareholders by maintaining transparent and up-to-date records with depositories.

body.title: Penalty Avoidance
body.description: Timely filing of PAS-6 helps prevent MCA-imposed fines, prosecution or compliance notices.

body.title: Audit Preparedness
body.description: Maintains streamlined and verified records, aiding faster financial audits and regulatory reviews.

categories.applicantType: The following companies must file PAS-6:
criteria: Whose securities are held in dematerialised and/or physical form
Regardless of the number of shareholders
Applicable to both equity and preference shares
Private Limited Companies and Listed Companies are not required to file PAS-6.

documents: List of shareholders in both physical and demat form
ISIN (International Securities Identification Number) from NSDL/CDSL
Details of changes in shareholding, if any
Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) of authorized director
CIN and PAN of the company

body.description: Collect half-yearly shareholder data from company records and depositories.
Match physical shares with electronic records using ISIN details.
Obtain a certificate from a Practicing Chartered Accountant or Company Secretary.
Prepare and digitally sign Form PAS-6 on MCA portal with DSC.
File the form and retain SRN for future reference.
Non-filing or delayed filing of PAS-6 may attract:
A further fine of ₹1,000 per day of continued default
MCA may issue compliance notices or initiate adjudication proceedings
Directors and officers in default may also face penal consequences

body.question: Who needs to file Form PAS-6?
body.answer: All unlisted public companies are required to file PAS-6.

body.question: Is PAS-6 required for private limited companies?
body.answer: No, PAS-6 is not applicable to private limited companies.

body.question: What happens if PAS-6 is not filed?
body.answer: Late or non-filing attracts a penalty of ₹10,000 and ₹1,000/day thereafter.

body.question: What if there is no change in shareholding?
body.answer: Filing is still mandatory. You must report “No Change” if applicable.

body.question: What is the role of Professionals Law in PAS-6?
body.answer: Professionals Law handles data collation, reconciliation, professional certification and MCA filing.

body.question: Do we need an auditor’s certificate?
body.answer: Yes, PAS-6 must be certified by a practicing CA or CS.

body.question: Can we revise PAS-6 once submitted?
body.answer: No, PAS-6 cannot be revised. Accuracy in the initial filing is essential.

body.question: Is PAS-6 applicable to preference shares?
body.answer: Yes, both equity and preference shares must be reported in PAS-6.

body.question: How can Professionals Law help?
body.answer: Professionals Law provides complete PAS-6 support—from document preparation to reconciliation, filing and post-filing assistance.
